content.problemStatement:
Write a program using `while` loop to check if the positive number NNN is prime or not.

**Note:** Prime numbers are the numbers that are divisible by only 1 and the number itself.


Input Format

The first and only line of input will contain a single positive number NNN.


Output Format

Output on a single line, `YES` if NNN is prime, else `NO`.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single positive integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print YES if N is a prime number, otherwise print NO. Each answer should be on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 4 is divisible by 2 → not prime. 11 has no divisor other than 1 and itself → prime. 25 is divisible by 5 → not prime.
1 is not considered prime. 2 is the smallest prime number.
3 and 9973 are prime. 1,000,000 is even and >2, thus composite. 999983 is a prime close to the upper limit.

solution.approach: A number N is prime if it is greater than 1 and has no divisor other than 1 and itself. For each test case we handle the trivial cases N<=1 (not prime), N==2 (prime) and even numbers >2 (not prime). For the remaining odd numbers we test divisibility by all odd integers i from 3 up to sqrt(N). If any i divides N, the number is composite; otherwise it is prime. This trial‑division algorithm is fast enough because sqrt(10^6) = 1000, so at most ~500 checks per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(sqrt(N)) per test case, O(T * sqrt(N)) total.
